Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (isbn INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title STRING NOT NULL, author STRING) CREATE TABLE Copy (isbn INTEGER, serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, weight INTEGER, bookcase INTEGER, FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE);

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author) VALUES (1234, 'ab', 'mr'); INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author) VALUES (1234, 'bc', 'ms');

Answer: UPDATE Book SET isbn = 1234 WHERE title = 'A First Course In Database Systems'; CHECK (isbn = 1234 WHERE serial_number = 2);

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = 9781292025827; CHECK (NOT EXISTS (SELECT * FROM Copy WHERE isbn = 9781292025827));

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy (isbn INTEGER, serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, weight INTEGER, bookcase INTEGER, FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE, CONSTRAINT correct_weight CHECK (weight > 10 AND weight < 1000);

Answer: CREATE ASSERTION widom_exists CHECK ( EXISTS (SELECT * FROM Copy, Book WHERE Copy.isbn = Book.isbn AND author = 'Jennifer Widom'));

Answer: CREATE ASSERTION max_weight CHECK((SELECT sum(weight) FROM Copy) <= 1000000000);

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION max_weight_per_bookcase CHECK(NOT EXISTS((SELECT bookcase, sum(weight) FROM Copy GROUP BY bookcase HAVING sum(weight) > 1000000)));

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: SELECT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM Copy     WHERE isbn IN (         SELECT isbn         FROM Book         WHERE author = 'Jennifer Widom'     ) );

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: ALTER TABLE Copy ADD CONSTRAINT check_total_weight CHECK (     (SELECT SUM(weight)     FROM Copy) <= 1000000000 );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: ALTER TABLE Copy ADD CONSTRAINT check_bookcase_weight CHECK (     SELECT SUM(weight)     FROM Copy     GROUP BY bookcase     HAVING SUM(weight) <= 1000000 );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;   CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn VARCHAR(13),     title VARCHAR NOT NULL,     author VARCHAR,       PRIMARY KEY(isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn VARCHAR(13),     serial_number INT,     weight INT,     bookcase INT NOT NULL,       PRIMARY KEY(serial_number),     FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION check_total_weight_limit() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$ DECLARE     total_weight INT; BEGIN     SELECT SUM(weight) INTO total_weight FROM Copy;     IF (TG_OP = 'INSERT') THEN         IF (total_weight + NEW.weight > 1000000000) THEN             RAISE EXCEPTION 'Total weight of book copies exceeds the limit';         END IF;     ELSIF (TG_OP = 'UPDATE') THEN         IF (total_weight - OLD.weight + NEW.weight > 1000000000) THEN             RAISE EXCEPTION 'Total weight of book copies exceeds the limit';         END IF;     END IF;     RETURN NEW; END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn INT , title TEXT, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY(isbn), UNIQUE (isbn) ); CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn INT, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY(serial_number), FOREIGN KEY(isbn)        REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE  ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn VARCHAR(13),     serial_number INT PRIMARY KEY,     weight INT CHECK (weight >= 10 AND weight <= 1000), -- CHECK constraint for weight     bookcase INT,     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn INT, PRIMARY KEY(isbn), title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT )   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn INT, serial_number INT, PRIMARY KEY(serial_number), weight INT, bookcase INT, FOREIGN KEY (isbn)  REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE )

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT NOT NULL );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn TEXT, serial_number INT PRIMARY KEY, weight INT, bookcase INT, FOREIGN KEY isbn REFERNCES Book (isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;   CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY,     title TEXT NOT NULL,     author TEXT );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn TEXT,     serial_number INT PRIMARY KEY,     weight INT,     bookcase INT,     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE LibraryWeight (     total_weight INTEGER ); INSERT INTO LibraryWeight VALUES (0);   CREATE TRIGGER UpdateLibraryWeight AFTER INSERT ON Copy BEGIN     UPDATE LibraryWeight SET total_weight = total_weight + NEW.weight; END;   CREATE TRIGGER DeletionUpdateLibraryWeight AFTER DELETE ON Copy BEGIN     UPDATE LibraryWeight SET total_weight = total_weight - OLD.weight; END;   CREATE ASSERTION TotalWeightConstraint CHECK (     (SELECT total_weight FROM LibraryWeight) <= 1000000000 );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE TABLE BookcaseWeight (     bookcase_id INTEGER,     total_weight INTEGER,     PRIMARY KEY (bookcase_id) );   CREATE TRIGGER UpdateBookcaseWeight AFTER INSERT ON Copy BEGIN     UPDATE BookcaseWeight     SET total_weight = total_weight + NEW.weight     WHERE bookcase_id = NEW.bookcase; END;   CREATE TRIGGER DeletionUpdateBookcaseWeight AFTER DELETE ON Copy BEGIN     UPDATE BookcaseWeight     SET total_weight = total_weight - OLD.weight     WHERE bookcase_id = OLD.bookcase; END;   CREATE ASSERTION BookcaseWeightConstraint CHECK (     (SELECT total_weight.bookcase_id FROM LibraryWeight) <= 1000000000 );

Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;   CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn TEXT, serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, weight INTEGER, bookcase INTEGER, FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(  isbn CHAR(13) PRIMARY KEY,  title CHAR(255) NOT NULL,  author CHAR (255)  ); CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn CHAR(13),     serial_number INT PRIMARY KEY,     weight INT,     bookcase INT,     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON; CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY,     title TEXT NOT NULL,     author TEXT ); CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn TEXT,     serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,     weight INTEGER,     bookcase INTEGER,     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)         ON UPDATE CASCADE         ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn INT PRIMARY KEY title TEXT NOT NULL author TEXT );   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn INT serial_number INT weight INT bookcase INT UNIQUE (isbn, serial number)  FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: Let's assume that we have two copies of Jennifer Widom's book, i.e., the table Copy has the following two rows:    [ ('9781292025827', 1, 200, 81),      ('9781292025827', 2, 200, 81) ] Give an SQL update statement that shows that condition 3 is handled correctly.
Answer: UPDATE Book SET ISBN = '12' WHERE ISBN = '9781292025827';

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = '12'; SELECT * FROM Copy;

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn_copy text,     serial_number integer,     weight integer NOT NULL CHECK (weight >= 10 AND weight <= 1000),     bookcase integer NOT NULL,     PRIMARY KEY(serial_number),     FOREIGN KEY(isbn_copy) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION copy_check CHECK (EXISTS (SELECT Copy.serial_number FROM Book, Copy WHERE author = 'Jennifer Widom'));

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION copy_billion CHECK (NOT EXISTS (SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy HAVING SUM(weight) < 1000000000;));

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION copy_billion_case CHECK (NOT EXISTS (SELECT SUM(weight) FROM Copy Group By bookcase HAVING SUM(weight) <= 1000000000;));

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;   CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn TEXT NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn TEXT NOT NULL, serial_number INT NOT NULL, weight INT NOT NULL, bookcase INT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn INT, title CHAR(15), author CHAR(31), PRIMARY KEY (isbn)); CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn INT, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY (serial_number), FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,  UNIQUE(isbn, serial_number));

The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(     isbn TEXT,     title TEXT NOT NULL,     author TEXT,     PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy(     isbn TEXT NOT NULL,     serial_number TEXT,     weight FLOAT,     bookcase INT NOT NULL,     PRIMARY KEY (serial_number),     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)              ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO     Book(isbn, title, author)     VALUES ('9789492182715', 'The amazing book', 'Sherry M. Holmes'),                     ('9789492182715', 'The less amazing book', 'Sheryl N. Hulmes');

Q Text: Let's assume that we have two copies of Jennifer Widom's book, i.e., the table Copy has the following two rows:    [ ('9781292025827', 1, 200, 81),      ('9781292025827', 2, 200, 81) ] Give an SQL update statement that shows that condition 3 is handled correctly.
Answer: UPDATE Book SET isbn = '1234567890123' WHERE isbn = '9781292025827';    SELECT isbn FROM Copy WHERE isbn = '1234567890123';

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = '1234567890123';   SELECT isbn FROM Copy WHERE isbn;

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: DROP TABLE Copy;   CREATE TABLE Copy(     isbn TEXT NOT NULL,     serial_number TEXT,     weight FLOAT,      bookcase INT NOT NULL,     PRIMARY KEY (serial_number),     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)              ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,     CONSTRAINT sensible_weight CHECK (weight IS NULL OR (weight>10 AND weight<1000)) );

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION Jennifer_Widom_must_exsist CHECK ( EXISTS (     SELECT *     FROM Book, Copy     WHERE Book.isbn = Copy.isbn     AND Book.author  = 'Jennifer Widom' ) );

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION total_weight_less_than_billion CHECK ( 1000000000 > (     SELECT SUM(weight)     FROM Copy     ) );

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION weight_in_bookcase_less_than_million CHECK ( 1000000 > (     SELECT SUM(weight)     FROM Copy     GROUP BY bookcase     ) );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,     title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,     author VARCHAR(255)  );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn VARCHAR(50),     serial_number INT PRIMARY KEY,     weight INT,      bookcase INT,      FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE );
